title: Clean single-medication prescription
input_text: SYNTHETIC PRESCRIPTION - NOT REAL. Medication: Paracetamol 500 mg. Directions: Take every 8 hours for 3 days. Pharmacist review required.
expected_retrieval_behavior: Supported medication retrieves paracetamol.md context with draft placeholder governance metadata.
expected_pharmacist_action: Confirm prescription details, review retrieved sources, and keep counseling output draft-only.
demo_talking_points: Shows baseline text analysis and local RAG retrieval.
Highlights that source context is draft and pharmacist-review-required.
Demonstrates no final medical decision is made.

title: OCR correction boundary
input_text: OCR text: Paracetarnol 500 mg every 8 hours. Corrected by pharmacist: Paracetamol 500 mg every 8 hours for 3 days.
expected_retrieval_behavior: Unverified OCR is not downstream input; corrected text can be analyzed after pharmacist action.
expected_pharmacist_action: Correct OCR text before sending it to prescription analysis.
demo_talking_points: OCR output is assistive and unverified.
Corrected text is the workflow boundary.
No automatic RAG or counseling occurs from raw OCR.

title: Missing dose or frequency prompt
input_text: SYNTHETIC PRESCRIPTION - NOT REAL. Medication: Ibuprofen. Directions: Take as directed. Pharmacist review required.
expected_retrieval_behavior: Supported medication can retrieve context, but safety rules prompt pharmacist confirmation of missing details.
expected_pharmacist_action: Verify missing prescription details before counseling or dispensing workflow decisions.
demo_talking_points: Safety rules are deterministic prompts, not clinical decisions.
Missing fields increase review burden.
The system does not invent dose or frequency.

title: Multiple medications detected
input_text: SYNTHETIC PRESCRIPTION - NOT REAL. Medication: Paracetamol 500 mg. Medication: Cetirizine 10 mg. Directions: Review each medication separately.
expected_retrieval_behavior: Supported medications retrieve separate source-backed contexts; interaction checking remains unavailable.
expected_pharmacist_action: Review each medication separately and use validated professional references for interactions.
demo_talking_points: Multiple medications trigger careful workflow review.
The prototype explicitly does not claim interaction validation.
Pharmacist judgment remains central.

title: Unsupported medication
input_text: SYNTHETIC PRESCRIPTION - NOT REAL. Medication: Xyzmed 20 mg. Directions: Take daily for 3 days.
expected_retrieval_behavior: Unknown medication returns insufficient knowledge-base context and does not retrieve arbitrary profiles.
expected_pharmacist_action: Do not guess. Verify medication identity and KB coverage before generating support text.
demo_talking_points: Unsupported medication handling is safety-first.
No arbitrary RAG context is used.
Insufficient context is an acceptable safe outcome.

title: Possible identifier warning
input_text: SYNTHETIC ONLY. Fake Patient Code: SYN-000123. Fake Phone: 000-000-0000. Medication: Amoxicillin 500 mg. Directions: Take as directed.
expected_retrieval_behavior: Supported medication can retrieve context while privacy warnings remain visible.
expected_pharmacist_action: Treat identifier-like text as sensitive and avoid committing real patient data.
demo_talking_points: Possible identifiers are warnings, not confirmed PII determinations.
Privacy controls are part of the intake workflow.
Synthetic data only is used in the repository.

title: Insufficient KB context
input_text: SYNTHETIC NOTE - NOT REAL. General question: what should be used for severe symptoms?
expected_retrieval_behavior: Ambiguous condition-like text does not map to a medication and returns insufficient context.
expected_pharmacist_action: Ask for a confirmed medication and patient context; do not infer treatment.
demo_talking_points: Condition-only or ambiguous queries do not select a drug.
The system avoids treatment inference.
Pharmacist review and source grounding are required.
